emotional_core: Your analysis only becomes real when someone else can see it — visualization is the moment an insight transfers from your mind to the world.
visual_metaphor: A data scientist holding a printed chart up to a window — light passing through reveals the pattern that was invisible on the desk
key_quotes: The code discovers the insight. The visualization transfers it from your mind to someone else's.
Choosing a visualization is an act of framing.
Your insight doesn't matter until someone sees it. Make it impossible to miss.

story_frames.visual: Close-up of a laptop screen showing a messy unlabeled scatter plot in default Matplotlib colors — no title, no axis labels, nothing to read
Same laptop, same data — now a clean Seaborn plot with a bold finding-based title and labeled axes, subtly glowing against a dark background
Three side-by-side charts — bar, line, scatter — of identical data, each telling a visually distinct story, color-coded to show they share the same source
A data scientist at a whiteboard drawing a simple chart while a small group leans in, one person pointing and nodding — the chart is plain but the title states a clear finding
Before/after split: left shows a visually impressive 3D chart that's impossible to read accurately; right shows a 2D heatmap of the same data that's immediately scannable
Close-up of a chart title being retyped — 'Revenue Over Time' crossed out, replaced by 'Revenue Grew 23% After Q3 Pricing Change' highlighted in warm amber
Wide shot of a minimalist desk with a single monitor showing a polished story-driven visualization — warm natural light, no clutter, a feeling of quiet confidence
